data.content:
I can see the problem. The target generates a `.cs` file at build time, but never adds it to the `Compile` item group. SDK-style globs expand at **evaluation** time — before any targets run — so the generated file is invisible to the compiler.

The fix: add the generated file to `<Compile>` and `<FileWrites>` inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation and register for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
I can see the issue. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates a `.cs` file during build, but it never adds the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The glob-based `Compile` items are evaluated at evaluation time (before any targets run), so the generated file won't be picked up automatically.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, and also register it with `FileWrites` for clean support.

Let me also invoke the relevant skill for this.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation and register for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes a `.cs` file during build, but SDK implicit globs expand at **evaluation time** (before targets run), so the file was never included in compilation.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="..."/>` and `<FileWrites Include="..."/>` inside the target's `<ItemGroup>` so the generated file is:
1. Picked up by the compiler
2. Cleaned up by `dotnet clean`
